title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: So today, I'm going to give you a tour of our cabin.

title: will / shall
examples: My grandma cooks very delicious food so we eat really well up here.
If you take a look around the cabin, you'll see all kinds of deer heads and antlers.
So we'll grill hot dogs, hamburgers, chicken, steaks, all kinds of delicious food on the grill.
I will be interviewing my grandpa who built this cabin with his bare hands.
So make sure to stay tuned. I will see you next time.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: I will be interviewing my grandpa who built this cabin with his bare hands.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: And I've been coming here since I was a very little girl before I could even walk.
As you can see, this cabin is very remote.
But sometimes it can be a little bit scary because if there's ever an emergency, there's no hospital nearby. You're out of luck.
In these days we can even watch TV here and make phone calls.
Even in the dead of winter, you can stay very warm in this cabin.

title: have to / must
examples: But you have to be careful sometimes because there is a lot of wildlife in the area.
So you just have to stay alert if you go for a walk.
So we had to go to the bathroom outside, which was not an ideal situation.
We don't have to rough it as much up here anymore.
Now I'm going upstairs. The stairs are quite steep, so you have to be careful coming up.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: This is our shed where we store our ATVs.
And when I was growing up, my grandparents would put a salt lick out there for the deer to come eat.
We'd always use binoculars and get a good view of the deer here.
When we would come in the winter time, it would be so snowy.
And we would sled down this little hill.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: So you just have to stay alert if you go for a walk.
If you take a look around the cabin, you'll see all kinds of deer heads and antlers.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: The whole thing is heated by this wood stove here.
They shoot the deer and then they eat the deer meat, which is called venison.

title: have something done
examples: We also have a bonfire pit where my family and our friends all like to gather around the fire, have some drinks and roast some marshmallows.
When I was very young here, we had an outhouse because they didn't have a working toilet.
